name: burp-parameter-names.txt
purpose: The baseline wordlist of HTTP parameter names from SecLists: the most common GET/POST/JSON parameter names for discovering hidden parameters. The de facto standard for most parameter scanners.
whenToUse: When you are looking for hidden/undocumented parameters on an endpoint and need a fast, time-tested list of names.

name: Arjun medium.txt (db/medium.txt)
purpose: The default Arjun wordlist (~25 890 parameter names), with which the tool enumerates hidden parameters in literally seconds. Balanced in size and coverage.
whenToUse: When you run Arjun without your own wordlist - this is its standard default list.

name: Arjun large.txt (db/large.txt)
purpose: The extended Arjun parameter wordlist - the most complete of the three (large/medium/small), for the deepest possible enumeration of parameter names.
whenToUse: When medium.txt found nothing and you are ready for a longer, noisier enumeration in search of rare parameters.

name: raft-large-words.txt (as parameters)
purpose: A large wordlist of "words" from the RAFT project, originally for content but widely reused as a list of parameter names and general tokens. Very broad coverage.
whenToUse: When you need one universal, sizable list for both parameters and content, without a separate param wordlist.

name: Param Miner / x8 (built-in lists)
purpose: Param Miner (a Burp extension) and x8 (a Rust scanner) look for hidden parameters and headers; they have no separate wordlist file on Kali - Param Miner carries a built-in list inside the BApp, and x8 reads a wordlist via -w or from stdin.
whenToUse: When working from Burp (Param Miner) or when you need a fast CLI run (x8) - you usually feed them burp-parameter-names.txt or the Arjun wordlists.

name: blns.txt (Big List of Naughty Strings)
purpose: The classic list of "naughty" strings (unicode, special characters, SQL/XSS/command meta-tokens, overflows) that often break input handling. Overlaps with the Payloads module - here it is just a pointer to the file.
whenToUse: When you want to quickly run an input field/API through edge-case strings for incorrect handling.

name: LFI-Jhaddix.txt
purpose: A collection of paths and payloads for Local File Inclusion / path traversal by Jhaddix (bypasses, encodings, typical target files). Overlaps with the Payloads module - here it is just the path to the file.
whenToUse: When you are fuzzing a parameter that looks like file inclusion/reading (?file=, ?page=) for LFI/traversal.

name: LFI-LFISuite-pathtotest.txt
purpose: A list of target files and paths for testing LFI (from LFISuite) - /etc/passwd and other sensitive Linux/Windows files. Overlaps with the Payloads module - here it is just a pointer to the file.
whenToUse: When you confirm LFI and need to enumerate which exact system files can be read.

name: Generic-SQLi.txt
purpose: A universal set of SQL injection strings for fuzzing parameters (quotes, boolean conditions, typical bypasses). Overlaps with the Payloads module - here it is just the path to the file.
whenToUse: When you mass-fuzz parameters for signs of SQL injection (database errors, response anomalies).

name: quick-SQLi.txt
purpose: A short "quick" list of SQLi payloads for an initial check of a parameter without a long enumeration. Overlaps with the Payloads module - here it is just a pointer to the file.
whenToUse: When you need to check a parameter for obvious SQL injection in a couple of seconds before a deep test.

name: XSS-Jhaddix.txt
purpose: A large collection of XSS vectors and filter bypasses by Jhaddix (tags, event handlers, encodings). Overlaps with the Payloads module - here it is just the path to the file.
whenToUse: When you test reflection of input into HTML/JS and enumerate XSS vectors through a fuzzer.

name: command-injection-commix.txt
purpose: A set of payloads for OS command injection (from the Commix project): command separators, substitutions, typical bypasses. Overlaps with the Payloads module - here it is just the path to the file.
whenToUse: When a parameter is passed into a system command and you test it for command injection.

name: special-chars.txt
purpose: A list of special characters for fuzzing - reveals escaping errors, injections and parser crashes when "dangerous" characters are substituted in. Overlaps with the Payloads module - here it is just a pointer to the file.
whenToUse: When you want to understand which special characters a field/parameter lets through and where handling breaks.

name: UserAgents.fuzz.txt
purpose: A list of User-Agent strings for fuzzing the header: testing UA-based logic, hidden branches and injections via User-Agent. Overlaps with the Payloads module - here it is just the path to the file.
whenToUse: When you fuzz the User-Agent header (filter bypasses, different behavior by device, injections into logs/DB).
